heading: Transcripts of records as evidence
label: 83 Transcripts of records as evidence
text: Transcripts of the records in the district land offices, when made and duly certified to by the Secretary of the Interior or such officers as he may designate for individuals, shall be admitted as evidence in all courts of the United States and the Territories thereof, and before all officials authorized to receive evidence, with the same force and effect as the original records.
notes: CodificationThe words “and receivers” which followed “registers” in the original text were omitted as superseded by acts , and , providing for consolidation of the two offices. See, also, Transfer of Functions note below.Oct. 28, 1921Mar. 3, 1925

Transfer of FunctionsFor transfer of functions of other officers, employees, and agencies of Department of the Interior, with certain exceptions, to Secretary of the Interior, with power to delegate, see Reorg. Plan No. 3 of 1946. See note set out under .section 1 of this title
